title: Refactor governance status derivation

sources.reason: Target for splitting governance status orchestration into focused helpers.
Existing behavior regression coverage for status derivation.

riskAssessment.reason: Behavior-preserving internal refactor with existing regression coverage.

executionDecision.reason: Contracted behavior-preserving refactor is ready for implementation.

preReviewWarnings: Confirm helper boundaries preserve recommendation, signals, evidence, and decision-driver output.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: derive_governance_status is decomposed into focused signal, recommendation, and evidence helpers without changing its output contract.
Existing governance compression tests pass and include an orchestration regression assertion.
Full AI Cockpit and project quality checks pass with no runtime behavior change.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: derive_governance_status combines signal collection, recommendation policy, evidence formatting, and decision-driver assembly in one large function, increasing maintenance risk.
constraints: Preserve the returned model shape and recommendation semantics.
Do not change runtime governance policy or unrelated scripts.
intent.rationale: Separating stable responsibilities makes future governance changes easier to review while retaining existing behavior tests.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.
